sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, values_list LIST<INTEGER>, nullable_col INTEGER);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, values_list, nullable_col) VALUES (1, [3, NULL, 1, 5], NULL);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, values_list, nullable_col) VALUES (2, [2, 4, 7, 9], 20);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, values_list, nullable_col) VALUES (3, [NULL, 0, 1], 15);
SELECT id, array_sort(values_list) AS sorted_list FROM test_table WHERE nullable_col & 5 > 0 ORDER BY id;
SELECT id, (array_sort(values_list))[1] AS first_sorted_value FROM test_table HAVING first_sorted_value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table AS t2 WHERE t2.nullable_col & test_table.nullable_col = 0) AS matching_id_count FROM test_table;
SELECT id, array_sort(values_list) AS sorted_list FROM test_table WHERE array_sort(values_list) IS NOT NULL AND id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE nullable_col IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY sorted_list;
CREATE TABLE another_table (col1 INTEGER NOT NULL, col2 LIST<INTEGER> DEFAULT []);
SELECT a.col1, array_sort(a.col2) AS sorted_col2 FROM another_table AS a JOIN test_table AS t ON t.id = 1 WHERE (a.col1 & t.nullable_col) IS NOT NULL ORDER BY a.col1;
SELECT id, array_sort(values_list) AS sorted_list FROM test_table WHERE sorted_list & 2 <> 0 ORDER BY sorted_list DESC;
SELECT id, (SELECT MAX(n) & 4 FROM test_table) AS max_and_value FROM test_table;
SELECT id, array_sort(values_list) AS sorted_list, (nullable_col & 7) AS bitwise_value FROM test_table WHERE sorted_list IS NOT NULL ORDER BY bitwise_value;
